## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is the pubcast shot. Oh boy. I'm John Loomer. Let's go. We need to talk about the learning phase. Oh yeah, the learning phase. So this is what happens when you've started a campaign, maybe you make an edit to a campaign and in a delivery column it'll say learning. So what this is is a period of time when Facebook needs to learn how to optimize your ads. Now it's not immediately known so they've got to basically test and experiment and figure it out. So who within your audience needs to see the ads? Where should the ads be shown? So which placements are working best? Who is actually converting? Where are they converting? So Facebook is testing and learning during that time. This is called the learning phase. So know that performance won't be stable during the learning phase because Facebook's still figuring it out so it's not going to give you the most optimal results until Facebook gets through it. So avoid turning off your ads until the learning phase is completed. Avoid making any big changes in reaction to the results that you're getting during that time. Your performance should be optimal once you exit the learning phase. Now understand though in order to complete the learning phase Facebook needs to get somewhere around 25 to 50 optimized events within seven days for that ad set. So if you've got multiple ad sets within a campaign you need 25 to 50 within each ad set. It's not a hard or fast rule. It's got to be 25 to 50 but it's somewhere within that range. It may happen sooner. It could happen later. Essentially it's the information that Facebook learns from and until it gets those 25 to 50 events that you're optimizing for you're not going to get optimal results. So the challenge becomes what if you're not able to get 25 to 50 conversions in a week and depending on the cost per action you may need a higher budget. You may need to optimize for something else. So if you end up with that learning limited message that's why Facebook's unable to exit that learning phase. If you're struggling to exit the learning phase consider consolidating the ad sets. If you've got multiple ad sets within a campaign it's making it harder for you to get that 25 to 50 conversions within a week or maybe raise your budget if that's a possibility or consider optimizing for something else that's easier to get that may be further up the funnel. Alright so hopefully that helps for more on the learning phase go to JohnLimmer.com slash learn. This has been a pubcast shot.
